cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The question is asking what happens if a person is "bitten by a travel bug." The options are A to D. First, I need to understand the phrase "bitten by a travel bug." From the context given in the passage, it says, "While some people prefer to stay in the comfort of their own home, others been bitten by the travel bug and can't wait to explore the world." So, the phrase is used to describe someone who has a strong desire to travel and explore. The passage also mentions that these people are drawn to exotic places and want to see different parts of the world. Looking at the options: A) prefers to stay at home – This is the opposite of what the passage says. The ones who are bitten by the travel bug don't prefer staying home; the ones who do are the "some people" mentioned first. So A is incorrect. B) feels a lot for pain – The passage doesn't mention anything about pain. The phrase "travel bug" is a metaphor for the desire to travel, not a literal bug that causes pain. So B doesn't make sense here. C) is eager to see the world – This matches the description in the passage. The text says that those bitten by the travel bug "can't wait to explore the world" and that they are drawn to exotic places. Therefore, C is a strong candidate.
